title: I Never Lost As Much But Twice,
content:
I never lost as much but twice,
    And that was in the sod;
    Twice have I stood a beggar
    Before the door of God!

    Angels, twice descending,
    Reimbursed my store.
    Burglar, banker, father,
    I am poor once more!
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "I Never Lost As Much But Twice,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
